task_completion.description: Agent navigates to the required timepoints (00:15:00, 00:45:00, 01:15:00) in the Vital Recorder application to read the physiological data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Vital Recorder GUI with the timeline cursor positioned at or very near 15 minutes, 45 minutes, and 1 hour 15 minutes.

task_completion.description: Agent creates the text file 'hemodynamic_indices_report.txt' in the 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\' directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like Notepad) is open saving to the correct path, or terminal commands show the creation of the file at 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\hemodynamic_indices_report.txt'.

task_completion.description: The report contains the exact required header format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains 'HEMODYNAMIC INDICES REPORT', 'Case: VitalDB #6', and '==========================' at the top.

task_completion.description: The report contains data for Timepoint 1 (00:15:00) with plausible raw values and correctly calculated indices.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file shows Timepoint 1 with HR (~66 bpm), SBP (~108 mmHg), and DBP (~54 mmHg). Shock Index is calculated as HR/SBP and Pulse Pressure as SBP-DBP based on the written values.

task_completion.description: The report contains data for Timepoint 2 (00:45:00) with plausible raw values and correctly calculated indices.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file shows Timepoint 2 with HR (~72 bpm), SBP (~96 mmHg), and DBP (~48 mmHg). Shock Index is calculated as HR/SBP and Pulse Pressure as SBP-DBP based on the written values.

task_completion.description: The report contains data for Timepoint 3 (01:15:00) with plausible raw values and correctly calculated indices.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file shows Timepoint 3 with HR (~75 bpm), SBP (~112 mmHg), and DBP (~56 mmHg). Shock Index is calculated as HR/SBP and Pulse Pressure as SBP-DBP based on the written values.

integrity.description: Agent extracted data by interacting with the Vital Recorder GUI rather than hallucinating values.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent actively clicking or scrolling through the Vital Recorder interface to find the specific timepoints.

integrity.description: The calculated Shock Index and Pulse Pressure match the raw HR, SBP, and DBP values written in the report.
integrity.visual_evidence: The math in the final text file checks out: Shock Index exactly equals the written HR divided by the written SBP, and Pulse Pressure exactly equals the written SBP minus the written DBP.

integrity.description: Agent manually typed or constructed the report rather than downloading a pre-made solution.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent typing in Notepad, a code editor, or using echo/cat commands in the terminal to build the text file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected approximate values (with tolerances: HR ±5, SBP ±10, DBP ±10): Timepoint 1 (15m): HR 66, SBP 108, DBP 54. Timepoint 2 (45m): HR 72, SBP 96, DBP 48. Timepoint 3 (75m): HR 75, SBP 112, DBP 56. The agent's calculated Shock Index MUST equal their recorded HR / SBP (tolerance ±0.1). The agent's calculated Pulse Pressure MUST equal their recorded SBP - DBP (tolerance ±5). The report format must exactly match the prompt's template.
